entries.title: Meta Shuts Down AI Image Tool Following Backlash
entries.summary: Meta has shuttered an artificial intelligence image creation feature after just three days, following public backlash. The tech giant last Tuesday (July 7) introduced a tool that let users generate images using public Instagram accounts. By Friday (July 10), the feature was pulled, after Meta received widespread privacy-related criticism. “Our intent was to provide a […] The post Meta Shuts

entries.title: OpenAI Safety Boss Resigns in Latest Executive Departure
entries.summary: OpenAI’s safety head is reportedly leaving the company amid a larger leadership shakeup. Johannes Heidecke, the artificial intelligence startup’s head of safety systems, informed staff last week of his departure, according to a Friday (July 10) Wired report. His departure comes after a reorganization aimed at melding OpenAI’s safety and research teams. In a memo […] The post OpenAI Safety Bo

entries.title: How to Scale Trust in Agentic Commerce and Digital Money
entries.summary: Joining the FinextraTV studio at EBAday 2026 in Copenhagen, Federico Aondio, Accenture Payments and Digital Assets Lead for Italy and Greece, discussed the future of agentic commerce. Federico provides interesting statistics surrounding the reality of agentic transactions currently and described the need for invisible payments and 'visible trust'. entries.title: HeirWealth switches on MCP server
entries.summary: HeirWealth today announced the launch of an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for its wealth management suite, allowing financial advisers, accountants, and family offices to interrogate consolidated client data through AI assistants using plain language.

entries.title: AI in Banking: What is Myth and What is Reality?
entries.summary: At EBAday in Copenhagen, Tapan Agarwal, Head of Payments Solutions and Krishnan Srinivasan (KS), President and Region Head, Europe and CIS Markets, Intellect Design Arena, discussed the myths and truths surrounding AI with FinextraTV. entries.title: Early AI adopters set to dominate European banking market - Visa study
entries.summary: Artificial intelligence will fundamentally reshape retail banking by 2030, with early adopters set to significantly outperform laggards, according to a Visa survey of European industry players.

entries.title: EU Wants More Power to Pursue Social Media Safety
entries.summary: European regulators are reportedly granting themselves more authority to police Big Tech’s consumer protection failures. As the Financial Times (FT) reported Sunday (July 12), this comes as part of a larger push by the European Commission (EC) to increase social media protections. According to the report, the EC is set to announce a proposal to […] The post EU Wants More Power to Pursue Soci

entries.title: Ripple CEO Says Company Considered Folding Before SEC Fight
entries.summary: Ripple’s CEO says the blockchain company considered shuttering following an Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) lawsuit. In a talk at the University of Kansas School of Business, Brad Garlinghouse said he and co-founder Chris Larsen weighed winding down the company and distributing its XRP cryptocurrency holdings to shareholders. entries.title: Will VoP Be Enough to Fight Fraud and Comply With PSR?
entries.summary: At EBAday in Copenhagen, Erwin Kulk, Head of Service Development and Management, EBA Clearing joined FinextraTV to discuss the impact of Verification of Payee on fraud numbers. Kulk said it was a strong introduction but no 'silver bullet' and said that fraud has not reduced, but in fact continued to increase. entries.title: ClearBank appoints Ken Johnstone as chief product officer
entries.summary: ClearBank, the enabler of real-time clearing and embedded banking, today announces that Ken Johnstone has joined as Chief Product Officer.

entries.title: Nium acquires crypto wallet Cypher
entries.summary: Nium, the global leader in cross-border payments infrastructure, today announced the acquisition of Cypher, a crypto-native non-custodial wallet and issuing company.
